_meta.purpose: Rubrics authored from LIVE DB assignment briefs, keyed by real assignment_id. Days 1-10 already have good live rubrics (NOT re-authored). Day 11 fixes the empty shell. Days 12-20 are new. scale_max=10 each; % = score/10*100 feeds the 35% submission slice. Pending user approval before grading + DB write.
_meta.convention: criterion = {key,name,max,anchors{0..max}}. sum(max)==scale_max==10. Where the live brief already embedded a point scheme (days 16 M3, 17, 20), those weights are honored verbatim.

skipped.day19_form_link: assignment e8191b09 'Registration Link - forms.gle/...' has no gradeable content; excluded.
skipped.day12_empty: assignment 52b89d89 has empty body_md but is a real duplicate image task; shares Day 12 rubric below.

day11_shell_fix.applies_to_assignments: all 10 Day-11 Intelligent Data Project tracks (group project)
day11_shell_fix.note: Existing shell had 5 criteria x 20pts but NO anchors (unusable by anchor-based grading). Rewritten to scale_max=10, 5 criteria x max 2, each fully anchored. Preserves the 5 original dimensions.
day11_shell_fix.title: Day 11 · Intelligent Data Project

anchors.1: Problem named but the 'why intelligent data' lens is thin.
anchors.2: Clear problem framing and why an intelligent-data approach is the right lens.

anchors.1: Mentions data but glosses over cleaning/missing values.
anchors.2: Honest treatment of cleaning, missing values, integration/enrichment.

anchors.1: A rule/model exists but logic is shaky or unexplained.
anchors.2: Sound rule or model that genuinely turns raw data into an intelligent signal.

anchors.1: Plausibly useful but not tied to a real user action.
anchors.2: A real user/operator would change a decision because of this output.

anchors.1: Write-up present but charts or roles unclear.
anchors.2: Crisp write-up, charts read at a glance, group + roles named.

red_flags: no group roles for a group project

day12.title: Day 12 · Lab · Image models + hero image

criteria.name: Same prompt across the image models

anchors.3: Same structured prompt across all four (SD/ChatGPT/Nano/Firefly), labeled grid.
2+ documented iterations producing a usable hero image.

anchors.2: A polished final hero image they'd actually use.
5-8 line note: original prompt, what changed across iterations, why the pick won.
Public deck, default AI output, no edits.
anchors.3: Public deck with the required slide structure.
anchors.4: Public deck, full structure, AND at least two cards hand-edited (headline + image swap).

anchors.3: Both hook+ask scripts OR a clear Gamma+Kimi+Canva workflow.
anchors.4: Tight 60-sec scripts for slide 1 & 10 produced via the multi-tool flow.

anchors.2: Specific: which tool earned its keep, which to skip next time.

red_flags: no edits over the AI default
no scripts and no tool reflection

anchors.2: Runs, one node failing or no summary.
anchors.3: All three nodes execute (trigger -> Firecrawl -> Groq).
anchors.4: Green checkmarks on all 3 AND Groq's 5-bullet output visible.

anchors.3: Screenshot of green nodes plus the 5-bullet summary (or bullets pasted).
Names the URL and which bullet surprised them — shows understanding.

red_flags: screenshot from the brief not their canvas

day15.title: Day 15 · Lab · Recognise/Try a ReAct agent

anchors.3: Colab trace run + screenshot.
anchors.4: Trace run AND the Thought->Action->Observation loop explained in own words.

anchors.2: Changed the calculator docstring, vague result.
anchors.3: Made docstring vague, re-ran, documented whether the agent still selected it.
Both agents with role, goal, and 1-2 tools each.

day16_milestone3.title: Day 16 · Capstone · Milestone 3 — Pitch + Live Demo
day16_milestone3._source: Honors the embedded brief rubric verbatim: Problem clarity (4) · Working live demo (4) · Real-world numbers/user evidence (2).

anchors.3: Clear problem + who it's for.
anchors.4: Sharp, well-scoped problem with a compelling 'why now'.

anchors.3: Live deployed demo, mostly works.
anchors.4: Smooth 2-min live demo from a deployed URL.

anchors.2: Concrete numbers or real user evidence.

red_flags: demo is localhost or a recording

anchors.3: Public portfolio: bio + 2-3 projects + contact.
anchors.4: All sections AND a visible self-made styling edit (colour/font/layout).

anchors.3: A 4-step plan, edited, then generated.

anchors.2: Tool + one nail or one miss.
anchors.3: Tool choice + what it nailed + what it got wrong and how they corrected it.

day17.title: Day 17 · Lab · Public repo + real API call
day17._source: Honors the embedded brief rubric verbatim: Repo runs after git clone (4) · API works without edits (3) · README has run instructions + screenshot (3).

anchors.3: Runs after clone, minor friction.
anchors.4: Clean public repo that runs straight after git clone.

criteria.name: Real API call works without code edits

anchors.3: Live API call renders real data with no code edits (and not OpenWeather).
What-it-does + how-to-run + screenshot.

red_flags: API key committed to a public repo

anchors.3: 3 rows (4o-mini/Sonnet/self-hosted) with live formulas.
anchors.4: Full column set (users/req/tokens/price) with a correct monthly-₹ formula across all 3 rows.

anchors.3: Cache-hit column AND a computed break-even cell highlighted green.

anchors.0: Cheapest price quoted as the answer.
anchors.2: Acknowledges stronger models for some tasks.
anchors.3: Maps which model serves which traffic; doesn't lowball with 4o-mini.

red_flags: cheapest price as the whole answer

day20.title: Day 20 · Lab · Context that earns its tokens
day20._source: Honors the embedded brief rubric verbatim: Specificity of conventions (4) · Before/after evidence (4) · File hygiene under 60 lines (2).

anchors.3: Mostly specific conventions across the 4 files.
anchors.4: Sharp, project-specific conventions in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/cursor rule/skill.

anchors.3: A concrete before/after.
anchors.4: Clear before/after: a question the agent got wrong without context, right with it.

anchors.2: CLAUDE.md under 60 lines, no fluff; all 4 files present.
